dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses classic golden cross strategy: SMA(20) crosses above SMA(50) for entry, reversal (SMA(50) crosses above SMA(20)) for exit. Mapped short_window=20 to sma_20 and long_window=50 to sma_50 per allowed indicators. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT but operationally LONG for entry/exit logic. Position state checks omitted as they are engine-level concerns, not condition-level.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses classic golden cross strategy: SMA(20) crosses above SMA(50) for entry, reversal (SMA(50) crosses above SMA(20)) for exit. Mapped short_window=20 to sma_20 and long_window=50 to sma_50 per allowed indicators. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT but operationally LONG for entry/exit logic. Position state checks omitted as they are engine-level concerns, not condition-level.

source_rule_text: Enter long positions when a short-period moving average crosses above a long-period moving average; exit when the long-period moving average crosses above the short-period moving average.
